meaning: n.(城镇的)河边地区,河边陆地
examples: Shanghai's planners were praised for restoring a riverfront quay to its 1930s glory.
New apartment blocks and office towers are rising along the riverfront.
Early risers perform traditional morning exercises on the Bund, Shanghai's famous riverfront boulevard.
We might've taken the Dequindre Cut, a pedestrian-only greenway, straight to the Riverfront.
In 1970, Minneapolis created the Riverfront Development Co-ordination Board to reclaim industrial land alongside the river.
